_meta.title: M4 — panes + settings (ui-split components-tier · master-detail + collapse:toggle + settings surface app-tier · schema framework)
_meta.traces: agent-app-surfaces PRD v1.2 §7 (M4) · PRD-G7/G8/G6 · ADR-0120 (F1 multi-pane · F2 shipped master-detail · F3 schema framework IN) · SPEC app-surfaces-m4 (SPEC-R1..R14) · LLD app-surfaces-m4 (LLD-C1..C16)
_meta.notes: PLAN mode (feeds the M4 build fan-out, multi-seat-sized). THREE dispatchable phases sequenced by group: Phase 1 = n1* (ui-split, the wave's hardest contract — the gate for everything else); Phase 2 = n2* (app-tier panes chrome, after ui-split ships); Phase 3 = n3* (settings surface + schema framework, phased shell→framework). n4a = SERIAL INTEGRATION (single-writer measure-size.mjs + app barrel + the layering trip-wire extension); n4b = the per-phase independent component-reviewer gate. Each new gate ships a biting negative control on a unique token (n1f whole-shape/drag/clamp/touch/RTL, n4a size, n4a layering). Behavior = the SPEC (SPEC-R#); build = the LLD (LLD-C#). Open LLD forks (F1 base class · F2 new pane-resize trait vs generalize value-drag · F5 push-through RESERVED · F7 sync store · F8 settings composes master-detail · F-catalog Split-row) are RECOMMENDED not self-ratified (LLD §8). F3 (dynamic panes) + F4 (aria-valuenow scale) are now SPEC-RESOLVED (SPEC-R2 M1/M2 + SPEC-R4), no longer open. Doc-review REVISE repairs applied 2026-07-10: H1 (SPEC-R3 drag drive restated to the synthetic-dispatch + stubbed-capture INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE, structural capture-continuity, re-test trigger — no 'real pointer capture' claim) · M1 (dynamic panes now a SPEC-R2 contract) · M2 (mid-drag mutation aborts the drag) · L1/L2/L3 (PRD+ADR version markers, F4 SPEC-resolved, layering-as-named-NC). Constraints: NEVER edit tokens.css or site/lib/__fixtures__/ (token migration owned by another seat — pin ROLE-level color mappings only); app NEVER imports @agent-ui/router (ADR-0115).

nodes.label: M4 — resizable/collapsible panes (ui-split + master-detail + collapse:toggle) + a schema-driven settings surface
nodes.justify: grouping — the milestone root; its four subsystems are the child groups (Phase 1 primitive · Phase 2 chrome · Phase 3 settings · integration)

nodes.label: Phase 1 — ui-split, the split primitive (components tier). The wave's hardest contract; the gate for everything else
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the N-pane resizable split control + its constraint solver + gesture + gates + catalog disposition

nodes.label: ui-split + ui-split-pane element — controls/split/split.ts: N panes along `axis`, N-1 control-managed separators (role=separator + aria-orientation/valuenow/valuemin/valuemax/controls, tabindex), controlled+uncontrolled `sizes` (prop-as-source-of-truth), keyboard resize (Arrow/Home/End, RTL logical) writing aria-valuenow
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; split.ts extends UIContainerElement (light-DOM, formAssociated:false), `axis` reflected literal-union + `sizes` a PROPERTY (array, not reflected), ui-split-pane carries size/min/max/collapsible; renders EXACTLY N-1 separators for N panes (control-managed, author authors only panes — slider-thumb precedent); each separator sets role=separator + aria-orientation(axis) + aria-controls(primary pane id) + integer aria-valuenow/valuemin/valuemax + tabindex=0 + aria-label REACTIVELY; keyboard Arrow(axis+RTL-logical)/Home/End/Enter(if collapsible) steps the leading pane and re-derives aria-valuenow, emitting input(live)+change(commit); controlled `sizes` is rendered+emitted-on but never self-mutated (ADR-0102), uncontrolled holds internal ratios seeded from pane `size` (equal-fill, normalize to 1); degenerate 0/1 pane + `sizes` length-mismatch reconcile WITHOUT throw (mismatch warns once); DYNAMIC PANES REQUIRED (SPEC-R2 M1 repair) — a MutationObserver re-derives the N-1 separators + re-normalizes ratios (sum 1) on pane add/remove after connect, and calls pane-resize.abortDrag() FIRST on a mid-drag count change (SPEC-R2 M2 — settle at pre-mutation ratios, the captured index is stale); imports only ../../dom + ../../traits (no deep packages/**/src)

nodes.label: pane-resize gesture controller — traits/pane-resize.ts: axis+RTL-aware pointer→delta drag with pointer capture (per-separator, nearer-handle gate), the value-drag/slider-multi lifetime pattern
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. pane-resize.test.ts; pane-resize.ts is a NEW trait (LLD F2 — NOT a generalization of value-drag; value-drag is 1-D/horizontal/LTR-only and must NOT regress ui-slider/ui-slider-multi): (host, {separators, axis, rtl, onResize}) => release; pointerdown on a separator → setPointerCapture → pointermove maps position along axis (clientX horizontal / clientY vertical, RTL-inverted (rect.right-clientX)/width when direction:rtl) to a deltaRatio since press → onResize(i, delta, false) live → pointerup/lostpointercapture/pointercancel → onResize(i, finalDelta, true) commit; per-drag AbortController bounds all move/end listeners (zero residue), host.listen scopes the pointerdown to the connection signal; pointer-id tracked (secondary pointers ignored); rect ≤ 0 ⇒ delta 0; cleanup idempotent; exposes abortDrag() so the element's MutationObserver can abort an in-flight drag on a pane-count change (SPEC-R2 M2). NOTE the browser gate drives this via synthetic dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent) + stubbed setPointerCapture (the slider stubCapture precedent — synthetic pointers throw NotFoundError on real capture in Playwright/WebKit); capture-continuity is proven STRUCTURALLY via the AbortController lifetime, not real capture

nodes.label: constraint solver — controls/split/constrain.ts: two-neighbor local redistribution, sum-invariant, clamped to per-pane [min,max] ratio windows (the pure DOM-free math, value-codec precedent)
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. constrain.test.ts; redistribute(ratios, sepIndex, deltaRatio, bounds) is a PURE function (no DOM): grows pane i by delta, shrinks pane i+1 by delta, clamps delta to the tightest of both neighbors' [min,max], DROPS the residual (LOCAL clamp — push-through to non-adjacent panes is RESERVED, F5), leaves all other ratios untouched; POST-CONDITION asserted: sum(new)==sum(old) ±ε AND every ratio within its bounds after ANY resize; contradictory bounds (min>max) clamp to min + warn; min/max resolution (CSS length → ratio against measured container) happens in the ELEMENT which passes ratio-space bounds in, keeping constrain.ts DOM-free

nodes.label: ui-split.css — controls/split/split.css: Container/layout size-class geometry (NO control height), divider thickness + a ≥24px transparent hit-slop, focus ring, forced-colors legibility, reduced-motion, RTL-logical
nodes.accept: `npm run check`/browser green for the CSS legs; split.css is single-file sectioned (ADR-0003): a :where(ui-split) block declaring ONLY --ui-split-* (--ui-split-divider, --ui-split-hit-slop, --ui-split-pane-min, --ui-split-key-step, divider ink + focus roles) then @scope (ui-split); geometry is layout size-class (geometry.md §five size-classes — NO --ui-height-*, the divider+slop are layout dimensions, geometry trip-wire); the 1-2px visual divider carries a transparent hit-slop expanding the pointer target to ≥24px cross-axis WITHOUT displacing pane tracks (WCAG 2.5.8); :focus-visible ring per the interaction-states law; divider is a REAL border/currentColor (survives forced-colors: active — no fill-only affordance; watch the */-in-comment pitfall); no resize transition by default (prefers-reduced-motion; disabled during [data-dragging]); RTL handled logically (JS drag does the physical inversion, n1b)

nodes.label: descriptors + contract↔props — controls/split/{split.md, split-pane.md}
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; split.md/split-pane.md validate against the ADR-0004 frontmatter schema, tier:layout, extends:UIContainerElement, attributes[] mirror `static props` (incl. `sizes` marked type=array/property NOT a reflected attribute), marginal: line present (measured at build); the contract↔props trip-wire equals finalize(UISplitElement)/finalize(UISplitPaneElement) GREEN

nodes.label: ui-split gates — jsdom behaviour + cross-engine whole-shape/synthetic-drag/keyboard browser smoke (Chromium AND WebKit; INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE drive)
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. split.test.ts (jsdom: prop→DOM mapping; separator count = N-1; aria-* on separators; keyboard step → ratio + aria-valuenow incl. RTL-logical + out-of-set axis coercion; the C3 solver's sum-invariance+clamp via the element; `sizes` length-mismatch reconcile) jsdom also covers DYNAMIC PANES (append/remove → separators+ratios re-derive, no throw — SPEC-R2 AC5) + MID-DRAG MUTATION (a count change during a synthetic in-flight drag aborts it, settling at pre-mutation ratios — AC6, biting NC: resize continues against the stale index). AND `npm run test:browser` exit 0 incl. split.browser.test.ts (Chromium AND WebKit, SYNTHETIC-DISPATCH + STUBBED setPointerCapture — the slider stubCapture INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE, NOT real pointer capture): whole-shape (N panes + N-1 separators non-zero boxes tile the container ±1px); a synthetic drag (pointerdown→pointermoves→pointerup) resizes adjacent panes with input-per-move + one change on release; capture-continuity STRUCTURAL (a pointermove after a pointerleave, before pointerup, still resizes — the AbortController-lifetime proof, biting NC: listeners bound to the separator's pointerleave lifetime); RTL drag inversion; clamp at min (biting NC: drop the clamp → pane goes below min); touch-target ≥24px (biting NC: remove the slop); forced-colors divider visible; keyboard Arrow/Home/End; each new leg carries a biting negative control on a unique token. Re-test trigger (deferred): promote continuity to real setPointerCapture when Playwright drives synthetic events as active pointers (the slider tests' standing trigger)

nodes.label: catalog disposition — a2ui whole-fleet coverage gate (SPEC-N2/ADR-0087): a Split container row OR a reasoned EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry, no residue
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. a2ui/src/catalog/default/index.test.ts (SPEC-N2) with the ui-split descriptor resolving — RECOMMEND adding a `Split` container row to catalog.json + a ui-split factory in factories.ts (parity with the already-bound Row/Column/Grid/Slider/SliderMulti/List — verified 44 components; the agent emits axis+initial sizes+a ChildList of panes, resize is host-owned as Slider's drag is); ui-split-pane is a structural ChildList item under Split (the CardHeader/-Content/-Footer precedent), likely NOT its own row (confirm at build); FALLBACK: a cited EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry (ADR-0117 ThemeProvider/app-owner-chrome precedent) IF resize can't be safely agent-parameterized; the whole-fleet gate is GREEN with no unresolved-control residue either way

nodes.label: Phase 2 — app-tier panes chrome (@agent-ui/app), after ui-split ships
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the master-detail composition + the realized collapse:toggle region affordance

nodes.label: ui-master-detail — app/src/controls/master-detail/: a docked list|detail composition over the M1 shell + ui-split, narrow drill-in, selection state/events, 0 bespoke split code
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; ui-master-detail (UIElement, @agent-ui/app) composes ui-split (list|detail) wide with 0 bespoke split/resize code (the split's own DoD is inherited), reflected `selected` (item key) emitting select/change, two regions (F8 — generic-region precedent recommended); browser (Chromium AND WebKit): wide shows list+detail side-by-side via ui-split (resizable), narrowed below the container-width threshold DRILLS IN (one view + a back affordance) — drill-in assertion bites on a fixed non-reflowing layout; imports only @agent-ui/components (NEVER @agent-ui/router or deep packages/**/src — layering grep)

nodes.label: collapse:"toggle" realized — app/src/controls/app-shell/{app-shell.ts,app-shell.css,app-shell-isolation.css}: the ADR-0084 reserved value becomes a user-collapsible region affordance, wide-layout unchanged, both grid copies keep-in-sync
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green AND the M1 app-shell suite STAYS green (no hide/stack regression — this edits SHIPPED gate-green M1 files); COLLAPSE_VALUES gains 'toggle'; a collapse=toggle region gains a keyboard-operable user-collapsible affordance flipping a `collapsed` custom-state + emitting a `toggle` event (allow-listed); the [collapse=toggle]/[data-collapsed] rules land in BOTH the base app-shell.css AND the injected isolated :host mirror app-shell-isolation.css (keep-in-sync), STACK/TOGGLE rules AFTER the hide rule (source-order caveat — a reorder silently inverts the fix); WIDE-LAYOUT-UNCHANGED invariant: an expanded collapse=toggle region wide is byte-identical to a default region wide (assertion bites if the toggle path alters the wide grid); isolation ON: the collapse holds in-shadow via the mirror (biting NC: omit the rule from the mirror)

nodes.label: Phase 3 — the settings surface + schema framework (@agent-ui/app), phased shell → framework
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the settings shell + the schema→form-out generator + validation wiring + the persistence store seam

nodes.label: ui-settings shell — app/src/controls/settings/settings.ts: sections rail + per-section panels + narrow drill-in, 0 bespoke shell CSS, selection state/events, composes ui-master-detail/ui-split
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; ui-settings (UIElement, @agent-ui/app) presents a sections rail + panel area with 0 bespoke shell CSS composing the M1 shell + ui-master-detail (F8 — reuse the drill-in), reflected `section` (active id) emitting select/change; browser (Chromium AND WebKit): wide shows rail+panel together, narrowed DRILLS IN (rail→panel + back) — bites on a fixed layout; imports NEVER @agent-ui/router (layering grep, ADR-0115); `schema`/`store` are PROPERTIES (n3b/n3d) — the shell phase can ship before the framework binds

nodes.label: SettingsSchema types + field→control registry + schema→form-out generator — controls/settings/{schema.ts, generate.ts}
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` green; schema.ts declares the typed VERSIONED SettingsSchema/Section/Field (SPEC §4) + a field-type→control registry (ADR-0065 swappable-pack precedent) mapping v1 types text/number/boolean/select/slider/date → ui-text-field(+codecs)/ui-switch/ui-select/ui-slider, an unknown type → a disabled placeholder + one-shot warn (NO throw); generate.ts builds, per section, a ui-form-provider wrapping one ui-field per field (label/description/for from the schema, deterministic ids — fieldSeq precedent) wrapping the registry-produced control with the default/store value applied, 0 app-authored form CSS (fleet controls only); an unsupported `version` renders an empty/notice state + warns (versioning load-bearing, NO crash); v1 FENCE (explicitly NOT modeled, routes to a later intake): conditional/dependent fields, repeatable/array groups, cross-field validation, async validation, async option sources, i18n labels, file inputs

nodes.label: validation wiring — controls/settings/validate.ts: Field.validation → the generated control's OWN validity, one timing source (ADR-0050/0051)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; validate.ts maps each Field.validation (required/min/max/step/pattern) onto the generated control's OWN validity — native constraint props where they exist, else a setCustomValidity write on a reactive effect over the value — so errors render through the control's existing user-invalid timing + the ui-field error part + the ui-form-provider aggregate (NO bespoke validation engine, ONE timing source — the ADR-0051 reactive-error law; the two shipped error-timing bugs are the precedent); browser: a required/min/pattern field left invalid renders its error through user-invalid + the provider reports invalid — assertion bites when the wiring is dropped (the field reports valid); a pattern on a non-text control is ignored + warns

nodes.label: SettingsStore seam + reference adapter — controls/settings/{store.ts, memory-store.ts}
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` green; store.ts declares ONLY the SettingsStore interface (sync get/set + optional subscribe, optional batch save — F7; async/remote sync OUT-of-scope, PRD fence); ui-settings reads store.get(key) ?? field.default and writes store.set on commit (per-field-on-change with dirty tracking recommended + a batched save() method), timing defined+tested; memory-store.ts is a REFERENCE in-memory/localStorage adapter for the demo/tests; ui-settings imports ONLY the store.ts interface, NEVER a concrete store (localStorage/fetch) — the seam is a contract (grep-guarded, SPEC-R12 AC3); no store supplied ⇒ renders from field.default WITHOUT throw; a reference-adapter round-trip (write→reload) preserves values

nodes.label: integration + verification (SERIAL INTEGRATION + the per-phase reviewer gate)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the single-writer shared-file wiring done once + the independent judgment gates

nodes.label: budget re-base + app barrel + size line-items + layering trip-wire extension — scripts/measure-size.mjs · app/src/index.ts · app/src/layering.test.ts
nodes.accept: `npm run size` exit 0 with the components family ceiling re-based MEASURED-AT-BUILD (provisional 32 KB gz; ui-split's per-control leave-one-out marginal within the standing ≤~2 KB MARGINAL_BUDGET_DEFAULT — the REAL gate) AND a @agent-ui/app line gaining ui-master-detail + ui-settings marginals with its ceiling re-based measured-at-build (M1 was ≤3 KB provisional); a planted over-budget number proves the gate bites; app/src/index.ts exports UIMasterDetailElement/UISettingsElement + the settings types/store interface; app/src/layering.test.ts's isAllowedAppSpecifier allowlist ALREADY forbids @agent-ui/router by construction (admits only components/a2ui/shared/local) — the M4 work is a NAMED negative-control (plant a @agent-ui/router import under app/src, unique-token NC, prove the EXISTING test goes RED), NOT extending the test to close a hole; the components layering.test.ts re-runs green over controls/split/ (inward-only, no cycle); measure-size.mjs + the workspace/barrel are the SINGLE-WRITER integration slice for this wave

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer gates — one per phase, pre-commit NO-GO (ui-split · ui-master-detail + the region change · ui-settings)
nodes.accept: component-reviewer verdict recorded GO (zero blockers; COMPOSE ≥4 AND REALIZE ≥4) on ui-split BEFORE the Phase-1 commit (the load-bearing whole-shape + synthetic-drag DoD — the overlay-wave discipline), on ui-master-detail + the collapse:toggle region change BEFORE the Phase-2 commit, and on ui-settings BEFORE the Phase-3 commit; any NO-GO is closed + delta-re-reviewed to GO

actions.label: lay out N panes along an axis with N-1 control-managed separators
resize adjacent panes by pointer drag — axis- and RTL-correct, with pointer capture
resize per separator by keyboard and announce the size via role=separator + aria-valuenow
distribute a resize between the two neighbors only, sum-invariant, clamped to each pane's min/max
support controlled + uncontrolled sizing (the prop-as-source-of-truth law for `sizes`)
give the separator a ≥24px touch target, a visible focus ring, and forced-colors legibility
describe ui-split's public surface in descriptors and pass the contract↔props trip-wire
prove ui-split whole-shape + synthetic-drag (INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE) + keyboard cross-engine (Chromium AND WebKit)
resolve ui-split in the a2ui whole-fleet catalog gate (a Split row or a reasoned allowlist entry)
re-base the components family + @agent-ui/app budgets measured-at-build; keep ui-split's marginal ≤~2 KB
arrange a master-detail list|detail over the shell + ui-split with narrow drill-in
realize collapse:"toggle" as a user-collapsible region affordance (wide layout unchanged, both grid copies)
stand up a settings shell — sections rail + panels + narrow drill-in — with 0 bespoke CSS
accept a typed, versioned SettingsSchema and map field types to fleet controls (with a v1 fence)
generate the section forms onto ui-form-provider/ui-field with 0 app-authored form glue
wire validation from the schema onto each control's own validity (one timing source)
persist through a SettingsStore adapter seam (the app brings its own store) + ship a reference adapter
keep ui-split components-tier (no cycle) and app never importing router (the layering trip-wires)
judge each M4 surface COMPOSE/REALIZE ≥4 before its phase commit (the reviewer gate)

edges.why: the pure constraint solver feeds the element's resize path
the pane-resize gesture controller feeds the element's pointer path
the CSS styles the element's panes + separators
the descriptor mirrors the element's static props
the whole-shape/drag gates exercise the styled element
the descriptor trip-wire is part of the element's gate set
the catalog coverage gate resolves against the shipped descriptor
the reviewer judges ui-split only after its behaviour gates are green
the size line-item measures the element's final byte cost after its gates settle
ui-master-detail composes the shipped ui-split element
ui-settings composes ui-master-detail for the rail|panel drill-in (F8)
the reviewer judges ui-master-detail + the region change before the Phase-2 commit
the reviewer judges the collapse:toggle region change before the Phase-2 commit
the schema→form-out generator binds into the settings shell (phased shell→framework)
the generator applies the store's values when producing the fields
validation wires onto the controls the generator produces
the reviewer judges ui-settings before the Phase-3 commit
the app barrel + size line-items + layering trip-wire close over the finished settings surface
